title: benthamia nivea subsp. parviflora
intro: Benthamia nivea subsp. parviflora, also known as the white Benthamia, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its white flowers and small stature. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Benthamia nivea subsp. parviflora are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat various health conditions.
